description:
Wrap a Precisely DIS Data Quality rule set as N Dagster asset checks.

Example:
    ```yaml
    type: dagster_community_components.PreciselyDISDQCheckComponent
    attributes:
      asset_key: warehouse/orders
      dataset_id: "ds-abc-123"
      rule_ids: ["rule-001", "rule-002", "rule-003"]
      client_id_env_var: PRECISELY_DIS_CLIENT_ID
      client_secret_env_var: PRECISELY_DIS_CLIENT_SECRET
    ```

The DIS dataset is referenced by ``dataset_id`` (the DIS-side
identifier, not the Dagster asset key). Each rule becomes its own
Dagster asset check named ``dis_dq__{rule_id}`` on the target asset.

asset_key.description: Dagster asset key the checks attach to (e.g. 'warehouse/orders'). Use '/' separators for nested keys.
asset_key.title: Asset Key

dataset_id.description: DIS dataset id — the dataset the rules are bound to in DIS.
dataset_id.title: Dataset Id

rule_ids.description: Rule ids to execute. Pre-resolved at build_defs time so each becomes its own asset check (individually alertable + retriable). Get rule ids from the DIS UI or the GET /datasets/{dataset_id}/rules endpoint.
rule_ids.title: Rule Ids

client_id_env_var.description: Env var with DIS OAuth2 client_id
client_id_env_var.title: Client Id Env Var

client_secret_env_var.description: Env var with DIS OAuth2 client_secret
client_secret_env_var.title: Client Secret Env Var

token_url.description: OAuth2 token endpoint (override for non-global DIS tenants).
token_url.title: Token Url

dq_base_url.description: DIS Data Quality service base URL.
dq_base_url.title: Dq Base Url

poll_interval_seconds.description: Seconds between run-status polls while a DQ execution runs.
poll_interval_seconds.title: Poll Interval Seconds

timeout_seconds.description: Hard timeout (seconds) for a single DQ execution.
timeout_seconds.title: Timeout Seconds

title: PreciselyDISDQCheckComponent
